Mitch tries to help him do so, even though finding the horse puts him in conflict with the man who now owns Johnny’s old ranch

programme.title: China 9, Liberty 37 (1978)
programme.desc: In China 9, Liberty 37, Fabio Testi stars as a condemned gunfighter offered freedom if he kills an aging rancher (Warren Oates). But when he meets his intended victim—and falls for the rancher’s restless wife (Jenny Agutter)—the deal turns deadly. Torn between passion, loyalty, and survival, the three are swept into a violent showdown where betrayal and desire collide. With raw emotion, striking landscapes, and gritty shootouts, this revisionist Western blurs the line between love and vengeance.

programme.title: The Brand of Hate

programme.title: Abilene Town (1946) Colorized : Randolph Scott
programme.desc: Randolph Scott stars in Abilene Town, a frontier drama of change, conflict, and courage. As homesteaders and cattlemen clash over land rights, Marshal Dan Mitchell (Scott) struggles to keep the peace in a town on the brink of chaos. Torn between lawless violence, shifting loyalties, and two women pulling at his heart, the marshal must decide where he stands as Abilene’s future hangs in the balance. With vivid characters, tense standoffs, and classic Western grit, this film captures the spirit of a town caught between the old West and the new.

programme.title: Paranormal Prisons: Portal to Hell on Earth
programme.desc: Who is the bloody figure wielding a meat cleaver, seen racing through the alleys of British Columbia’s Chinatown? Whoever he is, he has terrified dozens of witnesses, who have never been the same afterwards. The brutal history of Cornwall Jail, Ottawa’s most notorious prison, lives on as ghostly apparitions of tortured inmates terrorize modern visitors. In its heyday it was home to vicious criminals, and sadistic guards. Hangings, whippings and torture were daily affairs and spirits from that era still linger on in the maze of cell blocks and corridors. Many male criminals were hanged at the Northwest Mounted Police outpost known as Fort Saskatchewan, but only one woman. Florence Lassandro was dubbed the Mob Princess, and her spirit is one of many seen on the grounds and in the preserved buildings of this historic site. Journey through several of the world's most haunted prisons and experience real portals to hell on earth.

programme.title: California Paranormal

programme.title: Wendigo Demonic Evil
programme.desc: All over the world, strange yet true cannibal attacks have been occurring all whilst Zombies, Werewolves and Vampires are more popular than ever. programme.title: Nacho Mountain
programme.desc: Keefer has a talent. He can eat...A LOT. But it’s not until after getting fired from his job, finding his girl getting railed by a tranny and almost getting arrested for drug trafficking, that Keefer decides (with the help of his best friend Meegosh) to start an underground food eating competition. After the health crazed Mayor Fingstal and his cronies Officers Slivjack and Biggsley catch wind of it, all hell breaks loose. Then it all comes down to one final food battle from hell, THE NACHO MOUNTAIN! Set to a killer Stoner Rock soundtrack with loads of babes and crazy characters in the vein of Porky's and Meatballs, Nacho Mountain is sure to please those who have gotten beaten down, only to eat their way to the top!

programme.title: FishON | Spring Break on Mattagami Lake
programme.desc: Season 8 Episode 3 kicks off our first open-water shoot of the year as Paul Ethier and Jeff Shippam make their way to the legendary Mattagami Lake for some classic spring walleye action. With competitive tournaments on pause this season, the guys revisit their favourite early-season hotspots—areas they’d normally fish under pressure—now with time to explore, adapt, and enjoy the day. From structure-hopping to dialing in the spring pattern, Paul and Jeff put their tournament instincts to work for a fun, relaxed day chasing hungry post-spawn walleye in Northern Ontario.

programme.title: Airtime Ep41: Kayaking The Mighty Rivers of Africa
programme.desc: The world’s top kayakers travel Africa in search of the most dangerous whitewater. Shot over the course of a year, this episode highlights the travel and adventure of world-class kayakers as they embark on a “bigger and better” hunt for the largest waterfalls in a land where emergency services are virtually non-existent.

programme.title: Country Ball
programme.desc: Meet Kayleb Wagner, Florida’s all-time record-breaking running back from a football recruitment powerhouse state. On September 17, 2021, Kayleb shattered the state rushing record previously held by Derrick Henry—Alabama alum, now a Tennessee Titan and one of the NFL’s most dominant running backs. As a new superstar flooded with media attention, Kayleb was poised for scholarship offers, including one from Army that was later withdrawn. The reason? Despite his athletic and academic excellence, Kayleb has one arm. Join Country Ball to hear Kayleb’s journey through his own words and those of his inspiring mom, Sam, exploring where he’s been, what he’s doing now, and where he’s headed.

programme.title: Country Ball
programme.desc: The first part of the season finale starts now. Jesse’s back on his home turf of Clearwater, Florida, at Clearwater Academy International with not one, but TWO of his top players: Luca Stanzani and Dylan Djeté – who’ve both been playing football since they were little. Dylan’s a family-oriented player who got five touchdowns across his first two games in America. Luca’s been CAI’s starting quarterback since eighth grade and has both the brains and the dedication to go far. See just what these players can do in this episode of Country Ball!

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Back by unpopular demand, the infamous “Blah Line” returns—and it’s more brutal than ever. The contestants kick off the day with a hellish PT session that leaves bodies broken and spirits tested. Things only get rougher from there with a punishing stealth crawl over jagged rocks, grinding them down before they face Ken Shamrock’s infamous Lion’s Den training—a relentless gauntlet of pain, grit, and raw endurance. As Don Mann continues to forge a battle-ready team, the squad is thrown into breaching drills designed for high-stakes mission execution. It’s chaos, it’s calculated, and it separates the contenders from the pretenders. In the heat of the action, two standouts emerge from the ranks—earning the coveted role of designated breachers.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day begins with a powerful dose of perspective as contestants hear real-life breaching stories from American heroes—reminders of the stakes they’re preparing for. The team is then introduced to a concealed firearm headrest safe designed for rapid-response vehicle defense. Next, it’s straight into the action with a preliminary course of fire using GreenLight sights, where top performers are pushed even further with a bonus round to test speed, accuracy, and composure under pressure. The intensity ramps up during a sentry scenario featuring SABRE CO₂ non-lethal cartridges, putting their stealth and judgment to the test. But just as the dust begins to settle, a new CIA briefing drops—and it’s the most dire yet. With mission reality closing in fast, the contestants close the day with a crash course in S.E.R.E. (Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape) training, rounding out their classroom combat readiness with hard-earned grit.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: The rain simulator is up and running and the results are shocking. But where do we go from here? How do we fix what’s broken and return to farming with Mother Nature and not against it? Wayne Swanson in Chattanooga Tennessee has some answers. This special episode of Farm Hero highlights the important mission American farmers are on tackling to protect and revitalize our soil… with the help of some Swiss Army Bugs.

programme.title: S2E3 Bike
programme.desc: GoPro Bike takes you to the world’s most extreme mountain biking adventures. Watch Geoff Gulevich, trail master, tackle some of the gnarliest chutes in Williams Lake, BC, dropping in with local legend Cory Brunelle, downhill expert. Then experience some of the best riders from around the globe as they conquer massive mountains, perform jaw-dropping tricks, and push the limits of skill and adrenaline in breathtaking terrain.

programme.title: S3E1 Bike
programme.desc: GoPro Bike takes you on epic, boundary-pushing adventures. Watch GoPro athlete Kilian Bron, trail innovator, team up with big-mountain skier Victor Broquedis, powder champion, in Peru as they summit Cerro Blanco, the world-record 3,860-foot dune, and ride unforgettable downhill mountain bike and ski runs. Then experience some of the best riders from around the globe as they conquer massive mountains, perform jaw-dropping tricks, and push the limits of skill, speed, and adrenaline.

programme.title: S3E2 Bike
programme.desc: GoPro Bike brings you heart-pounding downhill action from the Mountain of Hell in Les Alpes, France. Watch GoPro athlete Antoni Villoni, downhill master, as he navigates crashes, weaves through expert trails, and powers down 7,800 vertical feet of snow-capped peaks, dusty singletracks, and technical terrain. Hundreds of riders take on this epic race, where only speed, skill, and courage decide who reaches the finish line first. The rules are simple: first one to cross the line wins...

programme.title: S4E1 Bike
programme.desc: When the sun sets, GoPro Awards recipient Nikolaj Juhlsen lights up the Austrian forest on his downhill mountain bike, GoPro MAX mounted and headlamp blazing. Watch Jonny Livorti take on a daring staircase ride overlooking the Italian Riviera, where speed, finesse, and bravery are essential for this epic descent in Liguria, Italy. Why hike when you can mountain bike? Experience jaw-dropping trails, fearless riders, and the ultimate daredevil MTB action.

programme.title: S1E1 Two Roads
programme.desc:
“Two Roads” is an original GoPro series that follows nine athletes and one coach who have committed themselves to the relentless pursuit of greatness. Each episode dives deep into an athlete’s unique journey, revealing the life-changing moments that shaped the path they walk today.
This episode features Olympic bronze medalist Kristian Ipsen, American rugby union star Carlin Isles — celebrated as “The Fastest Man in Rugby” — and his remarkable rise from track and field and American football to becoming a two-time Olympian in rugby sevens. You’ll also meet Allison Stokke, a record-breaking high school pole vaulter who went on to compete at UC Berkeley, and Miles Chamley-Watson, a British-American fencing champion whose career includes 13 team Pan American titles, a 2019 team world championship, a 2013 individual world championship, three Olympic appearances, and a 2016 team Olympic bronze medal.
